SAP Future of PMM Training
Chapter 7 Learning Guide
futureofpmm.com

Closing in a Committee World

Key concepts and takeaways from Chapter 7

Learning Objectives

  • Understand how each buying committee member uses AI to evaluate vendors
  • Map content to the specific queries each persona's AI will run
  • Anticipate and pre-answer AI-generated objections
  • Provide real-time deal intelligence that helps close

What Each Persona's AI Asks

PersonaKey AI QueryWhat They Look For
CFO"What are the financial risks?"Hidden costs, vendor stability, exit costs, ROI validation
CTO"What could go wrong technically?"Integration complexity, scalability, technical debt, roadmap
CISO"What's the security posture?"Past incidents, certifications, compliance, data handling
Champion"Will my team use this?"Adoption data, training needs, user satisfaction, change mgmt

Stakeholder Content Library

PersonaContent to Create
CFOPublished TCO models, third-party ROI studies, customer financial outcomes
CTOIntegration docs, architecture diagrams, performance benchmarks, API docs
CISOSecurity whitepapers, SOC 2 reports, compliance certs, incident response
ChampionUser testimonials, adoption metrics, training resources, community access
Key Insight: You're not selling to a committee — you're selling to a committee where every member has an AI analyst whispering concerns. Your content must pre-answer those whispered concerns.

Pre-Answering Objections

AI doesn't just answer questions — it raises concerns. Build an objection library that addresses common concerns:

  • Support concerns → Publish SLAs, response time metrics, CSAT scores
  • Stability questions → Share uptime history, incident post-mortems, redundancy docs
  • Implementation fears → Document timelines, success rates, methodology
  • Pricing worries → Provide TCO calculators, hidden cost disclosures

Enabling Internal Champions

Your champion will be asked questions in meetings you're not in. Provide:

  • Anticipated questions document — "Here's what the CFO will ask and how to answer"
  • Objection response scripts — Word-for-word responses to pushback
  • Executive talking points — Bullets for each stakeholder
  • Supporting links — Easy access to documentation to share

Deal Support Tools

  • Battle cards — Competitive positioning for champion arguments
  • Executive summaries — One-pagers per persona with their specific concerns
  • ROI calculators — Interactive tools for building the business case
  • Proof points — Curated references matched to prospect's industry

Key Takeaways

  1. Every buying committee member now has an AI analyst raising persona-specific concerns
  2. Different stakeholders ask different questions — map content to each persona's likely queries
  3. Pre-answer objections by creating content that AI will surface alongside concerns
  4. Build an objection library that addresses common AI-generated concerns with verifiable responses
  5. Enable internal champions with battle cards, talking points, and ready answers
